transcript.clean_text: welcome back to GT and racing in highlights of stage four of the Tour de France well what a stage we had yesterday Julian Alice Philippe on the attack into equity and there was nothing anybody else could do about it the flamboyant Frenchman carved out enough of an advantage not only take his third career stage win at the Tour de France but also his first ever yellow jersey as leader of the race and it's a lead that he should in theory keep today another long stage at two hundred and thirteen point five kilometers taking the perotin from ramp to nazi but with little in the way of lumps and bumps along the routes that said the wind could play its part and so too could the fourth category climb the cote de Mer on three point two K's at five percent which peaked with just 15 kilometers remaining apart from that wind where the conditions were perfect for a bike race temperatures in the low 20s weather unfortunately not many rides were interested in racing at the start of the stage not uncommon on sprint stages these days where the breakaway stands little chance of making it to the finish line but it was disappointing to see just three riders head up the road after just a few hundred meters of racing there we had two riders from wanti Gobert frederick back art and yoanna Fredo plus the Swiss Minh Mikhail Shah of Team CCC with the bunch more than happy with the composition of that break their advantage soon climbed up to a minute behind it would be the sprinters teams who took the responsibility to control the gap lotto sudo for Caleb Ewan de Kooning quickstep for Elia Viviani and eunbok bhisma but Dillon Gruner they're good despite the wind blowing across the course for much of the stage the bunch remained reasonably can't nobody obviously wanted to light the blue touch paper too soon that said it wasn't without incident this is Tony gallopin hitting the deck again he'd still not recovered from the injuries he sustained in a crash on the opening day ouch it looks like it hurt today's intermediate sprint was located at 67 cage to go back are taking the maximum 20 points ahead of his companions bhai for the second day in succession Elia Viviani was best to the rest comfortably crossing the line ahead of sunny cold brilliant Peters again who'd started a day with a 17 point lead in that competition over Michael Matthews with 50 KS to go the gap to the leaders down to one minute and 26 seconds from a maximum of three and a half and another Frenchman thibaut pinot is also experiencing problems on roots career puncture forcing a change of wheels had chased back to the pure tone with his teammates although thankfully for them the pace at this point was not super high where'd the bunkers beginning to bear down on them Shar decided it was time to go alone although he wouldn't quite manage that initially his attack would distance afraid oh but not back at soon after though he would manage to drop the Belgian - but it was going to be a mammoth task for him to keep the bunch at bay and in fact the catch would finally happen on that final climb the day that cote de Mauro Bora hands Goa and team Sun web looking to lift the pace clearly determined to put the hurt on the pure sprinters behind over the top of the climb it was Sun Webb leaning and then Lilian karmesan put in what I think I'll call a television attack with 11 case to go basically getting some airtime responses totaled the erect energy the Frenchman will enjoy around five kilometres of freedom but eventually get caught by the peloton already close to full throttle and being led by a lot of pseudo from this moment on it was a very very nervous Bunch GC riders sprinters helpers lead at men all in the mix and wanted to be at the front with speed of 60 Kate's per hour spectators encroaching the roads and Road furniture thrown in the mix it looked like we'd have a crash at any moments thankfully though everybody stayed upright and it was the yellow jersey himself a la philippe on the front with 1k to go the speed had been so high there that most teams have struggled to stick together but this is the curling Quick Step speciality they still had more cough and reach AZ in front of Viviani Alexandre Christophe tried to anticipate the springs of the Italian but once Viviani kicks he had the measure of the Norwegian and would take his first ever stage win at the Tour de France five years after his only previous participation and just two months after what was a hugely disappointing Jared Talya for him an absolutely brilliant win for Viviani and you've got to say a lot of that was down to a perfectly doubt from his teammates a job well done for the quranic' quick step you've now on two stages on the trot equaling the early success of the Dutch rivals young beau bhisma third spots on the potent would go to Kaleb you and Peters again would come into fourth place and interestingly the young beau bhisma pairing of Grune and vagin and two nursin were 5th and 6th on the stage slightly strange in that normally one would be working for the other but they clearly both did their own sprint today in the overall classification there's no change at all in the top 10 Julian ala Phillipe still with a 22nd advantage over the best young rider well fan art with Steven cries like a further 5 seconds back in 3rd for the sprinters that missed out today unfortunately it doesn't look like there'll be another opportunity tomorrow stage 5 starts in San Diego vaj and finishes in comer 175 and a half kilometers is the distance between the two and along that way there are a total of 4 climbs and included in that are the first two second category climbs of the race join us again tomorrow here on GSN racing to catch all the action
